summary: Cleaned up menu and game-mode code by replacing generic decompiler names and raw menu constants with semantic names, consolidating menu data structs into shared types, and renaming menu input/event helpers. The PR improved readability around major/minor scene dispatch, main-menu entry/exit data, GameRules fields, and menu selection enums without evidence of large behavioral changes.
what_changed: Renamed local variables in scene-control code from decompiler temporaries to domain names: `scene` to `major_scene`, `var_r27` to `minor_scene`, `temp_r26` to `handler`, and `temp_r25` to `info` in `gm_801A4014`.
Renamed `gm_801A43A0` parameter `arg0` to `major_kind` and local `var_r30` to `major_scene`, clarifying that the function finds and runs a major scene by kind.
In `gm_801B0FF8`, replaced `arg0`, `temp_r3`, and `temp_r31` with `scene`, `rules`, and `data`, respectively.
Replaced raw `MenuEnterData` field usage such as `x0`, `x1`, and `x2` with semantic fields visible in the diff: `menu_kind`, `hovered_selection`, and `load_assets`.

Mapped many raw menu constants in `gm_801B0FF8` to named menu and selection enums, for example `MENU_KIND_REG`, `SEL_REG_CLASSIC`, `MENU_KIND_STADIUM`, `SEL_STADIUM_TARGET`, `MENU_KIND_MULTI_VS`, and `SEL_MULTI_VS_10MAN`.
Renamed `GameRules` fields in `src/melee/gm/types.h`: `unk_x0` became `force_main_menu` and `unk_x1` became `bgm`; corresponding references in `gm_1B03.c` and `gmmain_lib.c` were updated.
Removed local duplicate/temporary struct definitions from `gm_1B03.h` and `gm_1B03.static.h`, including `gm_801B0FF8_arg0_x10_t` and `MainMenuExitData`, in favor of shared menu data types.
Updated `gm_801B138C` to use `MenuExitData*` and access `pending_major` instead of `MainMenuExitData` and raw field `x0`.
Adjusted `MenuExitData` in `gm/types.h` so it only exposes `pending_major` plus padding; the previously named `pending_minor` byte was removed or folded back into padding in the shown diff.
Renamed the menu input bitmask enum from `_MenuEvent`/`MenuEvent_*` to `_MenuInput`/`MenuInput_*`, including `MenuEvent_Forward` becoming `MenuInput_Confirm`.
Renamed menu input helper inlines from `Menu_GetAllEvents` and `Menu_GetEventsForPort` to `Menu_GetAllInputs` and `Menu_GetInputsForPort`, with call sites in menu files updated.
Improved `MenuKind` names by replacing ambiguous entries such as `MENU_KIND_7` with `MENU_KIND_EVENT` and `MENU_KIND_33` with `MENU_KIND_MULTI_VS`.

Added `MultiVsMenuSelection` enum values for Multi-Man VS selections: `SEL_MULTI_VS_10MAN`, `SEL_MULTI_VS_100MAN`, `SEL_MULTI_VS_3MIN`, `SEL_MULTI_VS_15MIN`, `SEL_MULTI_VS_ENDLESS`, and `SEL_MULTI_VS_CRUEL`.
smart_moves: Converted raw numeric menu routing data into named enums at the use site, making `gm_801B0FF8` read as a table of major-scene-to-menu-selection mappings rather than unexplained byte writes.
Kept struct sizes guarded with `STATIC_ASSERT`, including `MenuEnterData` and `MenuExitData`, which helps preserve ABI/layout correctness while renaming fields.
Moved from one-off structs in game-mode headers to shared menu-facing types, reducing duplicated type knowledge between `gm` and `mn` code.
Renamed input-related concepts from `Event` to `Input`, which better reflects that the bitmask represents controller/menu input state rather than a queued event object.
Separated menu category from highlighted option through `menu_kind` and `hovered_selection`, making future menu transitions easier to audit.
Used domain-specific names for major/minor scene execution code without changing the apparent control flow, lowering risk while improving future readability.
decomp_lessons: When a byte field repeatedly receives known constants, derive a named enum and update all assignments together; this produces more useful decomp output than leaving `x0`/`x1` fields with scattered magic numbers.
Scene dispatch code benefits from naming variables by role: `MajorScene`, `MinorScene`, `MinorSceneHandler`, and `MinorSceneInfo` are clearer than register-derived temporaries.
Prefer shared forward/header types for cross-system callback data instead of keeping local placeholder structs in static headers.
For layout-sensitive structs, semantic field renames should be paired with `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...))` so matching work does not accidentally change binary layout.
If a field's meaning is uncertain or unsupported, it can be safer to leave it as padding rather than over-name it; this PR removed an exposed `pending_minor` from `MenuExitData` in the diff.
Renaming helper APIs should include both the enum type and all accessor functions to keep terminology consistent across call sites.
naming_conventions: Use `MenuKind` names with `MENU_KIND_*` for menu categories, e.g. `MENU_KIND_MAIN`, `MENU_KIND_REG`, `MENU_KIND_EVENT`, `MENU_KIND_STADIUM`, `MENU_KIND_SPECIAL`, and `MENU_KIND_MULTI_VS`.
Use `SEL_*` enum prefixes for highlighted menu selections, grouped by menu family: `SEL_REG_*`, `SEL_STADIUM_*`, `SEL_MULTI_VS_*`, `SEL_SPECIAL_VS_*`, `SEL_TOY_*`, and similar.
Use `MenuInput_*` for menu input bit flags instead of `MenuEvent_*`; `Confirm` is preferred over `Forward` for the 0x10 confirm/advance input.
Name `GameRules` fields by gameplay purpose where known, such as `force_main_menu` and `bgm`, instead of `unk_x0` and `unk_x1`.
Name callback data structs by direction and owning menu context, such as `MenuEnterData` for OnLoad/load data and `MenuExitData` for OnLeave/leave data.
Use local variable names like `major_scene`, `minor_scene`, `handler`, `info`, `rules`, and `data` instead of register-derived names such as `temp_r31` or `var_r27`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The diff appears to be mostly semantic renaming and type/header cleanup rather than a matching trick; no explicit assembly manipulation or reviewer-noted matching tactic is present in the provided context.
Preserving struct sizes with static assertions is the main matching-safety tactic evidenced in the diff.
Replacing duplicate placeholder structs with equivalent shared types can improve type propagation while preserving generated code if the layouts remain identical.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the human text excerpt is empty, so no substantive reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `MenuEnterData` or `MenuExitData` to see whether the padding bytes received confirmed names.
Search for remaining `MenuEvent_` or `Menu_GetAllEvents` references to verify the input terminology migration was complete.
Search for remaining raw menu kind constants assigned to `menu_kind` or `hovered_selection` that could be converted to `MENU_KIND_*` or `SEL_*` enums.
Inspect later changes to `GameRules.force_main_menu` and `GameRules.bgm` to confirm the field meanings held up.
Look for PRs that further name `MENU_KIND_8`, `MENU_KIND_10`, `MENU_KIND_11`, `MENU_KIND_14`, `MENU_KIND_22`, `MENU_KIND_27`, or `MENU_KIND_34`.
